task_completion.visual_evidence: The Sugar Write application interface is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Add the title 'Galilean Moons of Jupiter' to the report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Galilean Moons of Jupiter' is visible at the top of the document.

task_completion.description: List the four Galilean moons with their correct diameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The document contains the names and diameters: Io (3643 km), Europa (3121 km), Ganymede (5268 km), and Callisto (4821 km).

task_completion.description: Sort the moons in descending order by diameter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The moons are listed in the exact order: Ganymede, Callisto, Io, Europa.

task_completion.description: Insert the corresponding image for each moon below its text.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Four distinct images of moons are visible in the document, each placed below the corresponding moon's name and diameter.

task_completion.description: Save the report as an ODT file named jupiter_moons.odt in the Documents folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or file manager shows 'jupiter_moons.odt' being saved to or existing in '/home/ga/Documents/'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Sugar Write GUI to create the document.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Sugar Write graphical interface, not a terminal-based text editor.

integrity.description: The agent inserted actual image files rather than typing placeholders.
integrity.visual_evidence: Visual presence of actual astronomical images rendered within the document, and evidence of an image insertion dialog being used.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to generate the ODT file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing commands like 'pandoc', 'echo', or python scripts being used to bypass the GUI document creation.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected file path is /home/ga/Documents/jupiter_moons.odt. The correct descending order by diameter is Ganymede (5268 km), Callisto (4821 km), Io (3643 km), and Europa (3121 km). The report must include exactly 4 images, one for each moon. The title must be exactly 'Galilean Moons of Jupiter'.
